title: CoapBuilder content_format: out-of-bounds slice on format value
description: In rust/lichen-coap/src/codec.rs CoapBuilder::content_format(), the code creates bytes = format.to_be_bytes() which is [u8; 2], then tries to slice &bytes[0..2], &bytes[1..2], or &bytes[2..2]. The slice &bytes[2..2] is an empty slice at the end bound, which is technically valid, but the pattern is confusing. More importantly, the match arms reference indices that don't match the created array: bytes[1..2] and bytes[0..2] are correct, but the code logic for format=0 case tries &bytes[2..2] which works but is semantically odd (should just use &[]).
close_reason: False positive: Re-examined the code. bytes = format.to_be_bytes() creates [u8; 2]. For format=0, &bytes[2..2] is indeed an empty slice (valid in Rust - a zero-length slice at the end). For format 1-255, &bytes[1..2] gets the low byte. For format >= 256, &bytes[0..2] gets both bytes. The code is correct, just uses an unusual pattern for the empty slice case.
